title: Coming from ggplot2
text: 1. |>, +. Layers joined base pipe. vellumplot plot ordinary pipeline, first argument every verb plot. 2. aes(). Aesthetics named arguments mark . bare column name mapped data; literal value constant. color = hp channel legend; color = "red" paints everything red. 3. geom_* becomes mark_*. layers, different prefix. geom_point() mark_point(), geom_smooth() mark_smooth(), . 4. plot spec, picture. vellumplot builds inspectable specification compiles printed, saved, made interactive. summary() shows spec without drawing .

title: Coming from ggplot2
text: Note spelling: vellumplot uses American color primary aesthetic name (colour/fill accepted makes sense).

title: Coming from ggplot2
code: # ggplot2 ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, color = hp)) +   geom_point() +   scale_color_continuous() # vellumplot vplot(mtcars) |>   mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, color = hp) |>   scale_color_continuous()

title: Coming from ggplot2
text: layering translates one--one; + becomes |> geom becomes mark. Note vellumplot marks carry encodings rather inheriting top-level aes().
code: # ggplot2 ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_point() +   geom_smooth(method = "lm") # vellumplot vplot(mtcars) |>   mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg) |>   mark_smooth(x = wt, y = mpg, method = "lm")

title: Coming from ggplot2
text: facet_wrap() facet_grid() keep names formula interface.
code: # ggplot2 ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, color = factor(cyl))) +   geom_point() +   facet_wrap(~cyl) +   theme_minimal() # vellumplot vplot(mtcars) |>   mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, color = factor(cyl)) |>   facet_wrap(~cyl) |>   theme_minimal()

what: A bar chart with a manual palette
title: Coming from ggplot2
code: # ggplot2 ggplot(mpg, aes(class, fill = class)) +   geom_bar() +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("#6b4f2c", "#8a7350")) +   coord_flip() # vellumplot vplot(mpg) |>   mark_bar(x = class, fill = class) |>   scale_fill_manual(values = c("#6b4f2c", "#8a7350")) |>   coord_flip()

title: Coming from ggplot2
text: Two things ggplot2 easily fall spec-first design. Inspect plot drawing . plot data, summary() prints structure: Make interactive free. spec, handed as_widget(), becomes HTML widget. interactive channels (tooltip, data_id) mark arguments:
code: vplot(mtcars) |>   mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, color = hp) |>   summary() #> <PlotSpec> 32x11 (11 columns), page 6x4 in #> #> ── layers #> • mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, color = hp) df <- data.frame(wt = mtcars$wt, mpg = mtcars$mpg, model = rownames(mtcars))  vplot(df) |>   mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, tooltip = model, data_id = model) |>   as_widget()

title: Coming from ggplot2
text: ggplot2 habits carry , purpose: global aes() shared across layers; mark states encodings. verbose multi-layer plots removes inheritance rules keep head. + chaining operator methods gone; everything function pipe. Saving render_plot(), ggsave(), one spec renders PNG, SVG, PDF object. See one scene, three outputs. exhaustive list marks, scales, coords, themes, see vellumplot reference.

what: The one constraint everything follows from
title: vellum’s design principles
text: grid’s friction traces back single fact: graphical object’s size content known device viewport exist draw time. Text measurable width device open; native units need viewport scale; flexible (null) lengths need layout context. one constraint forces lazy units, deferred multi-method grob-drawing protocol, replay whole display list every resize. vellum’s central bet remove constraint, downstream complexity dissolves . way remove make metrics available construction time, means controlling measurement rendering stack rather deferring whatever device happens current.

what: Do the work in Rust, keep R declarative
title: vellum’s design principles
text: two things “grid-like framework Rust backend” mean. One graphics device: let R’s engine grid stay charge Rust rasterize primitives handed . low-risk whole ecosystem renders free, fixes none layout unit problems, grid still work upstream. reimplement model Rust (scene graph, units, viewports, layout, rendering) expose thin declarative R API. vellum takes second path. scene graph, unit resolution, layout, rendering live Rust; R describes draw. separate, optional grid--vellum translator (as_vellum() / render_grid()) gives ecosystem reach first approach secondary mode, existing grid, ggplot2, lattice output can render backend. Controlling stack buys properties rest principles depend : text measurable front, layout solved ahead drawing, identical output across machines.

title: vellum’s design principles
text: scene tree inert data nodes. node carries identity, local transform, geometry, resolved style, children, drawing code. Nodes immutable values: “editing” scene produces new tree shares structure old one, keeps edits cheap makes scene trivial inspect. addresses “missing middle layer” critique. tree retained R data, querying editing built scene name ordinary R, surgery internal layout table. grid.force() step materialise drawable form first; built scene already form.
code: library(vellum)  scene <- vl_scene(width = 6, height = 4) |>   push(vl_viewport(xscale = c(0, 10), yscale = c(0, 100))) |>   draw(rect_grob(name = "panel-bg", gp = vl_gpar(fill = "grey95", col = NA))) |>   draw(lines_grob(x = vl_unit(1:9, "native"),                   y = vl_unit(c(10, 40, 35, 80, 60, 90, 55, 70, 30), "native"),                   gp = vl_gpar(col = "steelblue", lwd = 2)))  node_names(scene)                                    # inspect the tree scene <- edit_node(scene, "panel-bg", gp = vl_gpar(fill = "grey90"))

title: vellum’s design principles
text: grid navigates regions mutable push/pop stack, drawing call’s meaning depends invisible current state. vellum threads context functionally instead: push() draw() pass scene along pipeline, navigate node name rather global “current viewport”. viewport model, regions coordinate systems, kept, one grid’s best ideas; mutable stack made fragile . stateful convenience layer can still built top interactive use, foundation.

what: Eager metrics and an explicit layout pass
title: vellum’s design principles
text: text measurable construction, layout can solved explicit, pure pass keyed device size viewport scales, result cached. Resizing becomes relayout plus re-raster, rather replaying interpreted R. performance story predictability story : inputs always produce layout, layout value can compute without drawing.

what: A small, principled unit set, and an honest trade-off
title: vellum’s design principles
text: grid’s unit system powerful sprawling. vellum keeps power matters trims set principled handful: normalized (npc), absolute (mm, cm, , pt), user-scale (native), font-relative (char, line), object-relative (strwidth, grobwidth, …), proper flexible-length type layout instead relative unit bolted onto absolute one. Object-relative units resolve millimetres construction, using shaping metrics text. trade-deliberate worth stating plainly, thing grid user likely trip . stored unit flat (value, code), arithmetic-expression tree. + - resolve construction: combine units code, two absolute units absolute code (vl_unit(10, "mm") + vl_unit(1, "") becomes 35.4 mm). deferred case, mixing normalized native code absolute one vl_unit(1, "npc") - vl_unit(2, "mm"), error, reduced flat value without device viewport. grid supports deferring sum draw time; vellum declines , flat representation exactly makes resolution cheap re-runnable resize. cost real: compose offsets viewport level, pre-resolve absolute units. case vellum trades piece grid’s convenience architecture everything else rests .

what: One extension mechanism instead of four
title: vellum’s design principles
text: Writing custom grid grob can mean implementing several hooks (makeContext(), makeContent(), drawDetails(), widthDetails()) different facts object become available different times. metrics known front, machinery unnecessary. custom vellum element function returns concrete child primitives , construction. one way extend system, protocol learn.

title: vellum’s design principles
text: Rendering runs -process controlled rasterizer, scene produces identical pixels every operating system CI. makes output reproducible snapshot-testable, property awkward guarantee result depends whichever device current. Text hard part, fidelity means reusing R’s font stack rather reinventing . vellum resolves shapes text systemfonts/textshaping machinery ragg svglite use, font chosen glyph positions match rest ecosystem; Rust glyph library supplies outlines get filled embedded. Matching ecosystem requirement, nice--: output drifts ggplot-via-ragg trustworthy.

what: One scene, many backends, and visible degradation
title: vellum’s design principles
text: Rendering visitor resolved scene behind single trait, PNG, SVG, PDF share one walk tree: critique notes device differences leak result. vellum abolish backend differences (raster fast path vector format differ), adopts principle : backend honour something, render raises one warning naming gap, rather silently producing different output. Unsupported features fail visibly. intrinsic fast-path trade-offs (marker sprite snapping high densities, quantised glyph-bitmap cache) documented rather warned, deliberate speed trades, failures.
code: render(scene, "plot.png")   # raster render(scene, "plot.svg")   # vector render(scene, "plot.pdf")   # vector

what: Performance is part of the model
title: vellum’s design principles
text: Matching mature C-backed system automatic retained-mode design; earned. vellum builds performance model: batched primitives thousand points thousand heavyweight objects, persistent glyph cache, repaint-boundary sub-raster cache object-identity render cache unchanged scenes subtrees reused rather redrawn, aggregate--shade path (datashade()) bins millions points density raster size output rather drawing one. distinction makes work semantic layer optimized rendering representation: million-point scatter need million objects.

what: What the retained model makes possible
title: vellum’s design principles
text: capabilities separate features much consequences keeping scene. tree retained re-renderable, vellum can pick topmost object point (hit_test()), something grid never offered beyond grid.locator(). reason, nodes can carry semantic identity survives SVG output, useful export, testing, accessibility independent interactive layer. every node resolves known transform bounding box, engine data draw layout (bounding boxes, viewport regions, clip chains) debugging overlay, explain given node ended size . Turning resolved scene coordinate person can see direct answer critique’s central complaint introspection archaeology.

what: What vellum deliberately does not do
title: vellum’s design principles
text: Scope discipline principle right. vellum ship grammar graphics: scales, geoms, facets; vellumplot’s job. include general constraint solver, flex solver plus measured tracks covers layouts arise, without large opaque dependency. implement CSS-style selector engine, cascading themes grammar concern. case elaborate mechanism sit wrong layer. Keeping low-level layer small lets grammar stay coherent.

title: vellum’s design principles
text: None free. Rust backend build dependency maintenance commitment. flat unit representation gives grid convenience. Reimplementing model rather wrapping device means inheriting existing ecosystem automatically (hence translator bridge). deliberate trades, made service one goal: keep strengths critique credits grid (grobs, real layout engine, nested coordinate systems, device independence) removing single constraint made rest hard.

title: The vellum ecosystem
text: parchment, pen, annotation name: important word compiles. vellumplot plot drawn build ; inspectable specification turned vellum scene printed, rendered, handed vellumwidget. Everything therefore one scene passing stack.

title: The vellum ecosystem
text: vellum foundation. describe scene small declarative API render ; scene graph, layout, drawing run Rust, scene renders raster vector.  reach vellum directly build bespoke visual, target higher-level tool, exactly vellumplot .
code: vl_scene(width = 4, height = 2.4, bg = "white") |>   draw(rect_grob(     width = 0.9, height = 0.8,     gp = vl_gpar(fill = linear_gradient(c("#6b4f2c", "#c9a86a")), col = NA)   )) |>   draw(text_grob(     "vellum", x = 0.5, y = 0.5,     gp = vl_gpar(fontsize = 44, col = "white", fontface = "bold")   ))

title: The vellum ecosystem
text: vellumplot gives grammar: data , marks scales declared tidy evaluation, everything trained laid .  plot spec, can inspect without drawing anything:
code: vplot(mtcars) |>   mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, color = hp) |>   scale_color_continuous() summary(vplot(mtcars) |> mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, color = hp)) #> <PlotSpec> 32x11 (11 columns), page 6x4 in #> #> ── layers #> • mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, color = hp)

title: The vellum ecosystem
text: vellumwidget terminal: hand vellumplot plot (bare vellum scene) compiles scene, emits SVG plus per-element table, returns htmlwidget hover tooltips, selection, brushing, pan/zoom, Shiny server. tooltip data_id arguments declared vellumplot, read vellumwidget, carried vellum scene: three layers agreeing one contract.
code: df <- data.frame(wt = mtcars$wt, mpg = mtcars$mpg, model = rownames(mtcars))  vplot(df) |>   mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, tooltip = model, data_id = model) |>   as_widget()

title: The vellum ecosystem
text: vellumverse front door; package documents full: vellum: grobs, units, viewports, paint model, datashade(), grid interop. vellumplot: full mark, scale, facet, coord, theme reference. vellumwidget: interaction options, linked views, crosstalk interop.

what: 1. Reusable drawing: grob factories and scene helpers
title: Extending the ecosystem
text: two ways package piece drawing reuse, ordinary R functions: nothing register, nothing teach renderer. first grob factory: function returns single grob. datashade() exactly : returns one raster_grob, function returns grob first-class primitive drops straight draw(), inherits graphical parameters viewport, renders every backend. second, drawing one primitive, scene helper: function takes scene, draws , returns scene. draw() takes one grob time, compose several primitives reusable unit still chains pipe:  Scene-, scene-means helper composes draw(), push(), pop() like built-verb.
code: # a labelled marker: a filled circle with text centred on it marker <- function(scene, x, y, label, fill = "#6b4f2c") {   scene |>     draw(circle_grob(x = x, y = y, r = 0.06, gp = vl_gpar(fill = fill, col = NA))) |>     draw(text_grob(label, x = x, y = y,                    gp = vl_gpar(col = "white", fontface = "bold", fontsize = 12))) }  vl_scene(4, 2, bg = "white") |>   marker(0.25, 0.5, "A") |>   marker(0.5, 0.5, "B", fill = "#8a7350") |>   marker(0.75, 0.5, "C")

what: 2. Custom themes: restyle without new machinery
title: Extending the ecosystem
text: grammar layer, theme function sets styling plot spec. vellumplot gives theme() individual elements set_theme() common surfaces, built element_text(), element_line(), element_rect(). reusable theme function applies :  Since theme ordinary verb pipe, composes everything else can shared one-function package.
code: theme_parchment <- function(plot) {   plot |>     set_theme(       panel_bg  = "#faf5ea",       grid_col  = "#e6dcc4",       label_col = "#4a371f"     ) |>     theme(       plot.title     = element_text(family = "serif", size = 16),       legend.position = "bottom"     ) }  vplot(mtcars) |>   mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, color = factor(cyl)) |>   theme_parchment()

title: Extending the ecosystem
text: seam ties three layers together, useful one understand build tools top. Interactivity feature vellumwidget alone; contract carried scene: vellumplot mark declares reserved channels tooltip, data_id, hover_group, hover_color, selected_color. compiled onto vellum grobs key (identity) meta (rest). vellumwidget reads back compiled scene (via scene_model()) wires widget. practical consequence: can make bespoke vellum scene interactive without touching vellumwidget , just attaching key meta grobs. Every primitive grob accepts . contract lives scene, mark invent vellumplot sets tooltip/data_id becomes interactive vellumwidget automatically, grob build vellum sets key/meta . Neither end know .
code: # a hand-built scene whose points are hoverable and selectable s <- vl_scene(4, 3, bg = "white") |>   push(vl_viewport(xscale = c(0, 10), yscale = c(0, 10))) |>   draw(points_grob(     x = vl_unit(c(2, 5, 8), "native"),     y = vl_unit(c(3, 7, 4), "native"),     gp = vl_gpar(fill = "#6b4f2c"),     key = c("a", "b", "c"),                     # identity for selection     meta = list(tooltip = c("Alpha", "Beta", "Gamma"))   ))  as_widget(s)   # tooltips and selection work, no vellumplot involved

what: 4. Teach the ecosystem about your object
title: Extending the ecosystem
text: deepest seam as_vellum_scene(), S7 generic every consumer calls turn something renderable scene. vellumplot registers method plot spec; render() as_widget() accept vellumplot plot . object (model, spatial structure, domain-specific plot type), can register method compiles vellum scene, moment renders becomes interactive machinery everything else. Registering one method makes render(), render_plot(), as_widget() accept object; never see my_plot, scene compiles .
code: library(S7)  # a tiny custom object my_plot <- function(data) structure(list(data = data), class = "my_plot")  # teach the ecosystem how to compile it into a vellum scene method(as_vellum_scene, new_S3_class("my_plot")) <- function(x, ...) {   vl_scene(4, 3, bg = "white") |>     push(vl_viewport(xscale = range(x$data$x), yscale = range(x$data$y))) |>     draw(points_grob(vl_unit(x$data$x, "native"), vl_unit(x$data$y, "native"),                      gp = vl_gpar(fill = "#6b4f2c"))) }  p <- my_plot(data.frame(x = 1:10, y = (1:10)^2))  render(p, "custom.png")   # works: render() compiles via as_vellum_scene() as_widget(p)              # works: vellumwidget compiles via the same seam

title: Extending the ecosystem
text: four seams line four layers reuse: independent: composite grob needs theme, theme needs custom object, custom object gets rendering interactivity free moment can produce scene. independence point splitting ecosystem layers. See vellum ecosystem vellum’s design principles reasoning behind split.

title: Getting started
text: ecosystem compiles Rust crate (inside vellum), need Rust toolchain (cargo/rustc) machine alongside R. place: vellumverse meta-package. Attaching attaches three packages real work, single call puts whole grammar search path: now vplot() marks (vellumplot), scene rendering primitives (vellum), as_widget() (vellumwidget).
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("r-vellum/vellumverse") library(vellumverse) #> ── Attaching packages ───────────────────────────────────── vellumverse 0.3.0 #> ── #> ✔ vellum 0.4.0 ✔ vellumplot 0.5.0 #> ✔ vellumwidget 0.5.0 #> ── Conflicts ──────────────────────────────────────── vellumverse_conflicts() ── #> ✖ vellumplot::linear_gradient masks vellum #> ✖ vellumplot::md              masks vellum #> ✖ vellumplot::radial_gradient masks vellum #> ✖ vellumplot::sketch          masks vellum

title: Getting started
text: Every plot starts vplot() data frame, adds mark. mark maps columns visual channels (position, colour, size), arguments ordinary column names, evaluated data:  aes() +. Aesthetics named arguments mark, layers joined base pipe |>.
code: vplot(mtcars) |>   mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg)

title: Getting started
text: Give aesthetic column becomes channel legend; give bare value becomes constant. color = hp channel, trained continuous scale:  Compare fixed colour, draws every point adds legend:  rule worth remembering: symbol expression (hp, factor(cyl)) data; literal ("#6b4f2c", 3) constant.
code: vplot(mtcars) |>   mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, color = hp) |>   scale_color_continuous() vplot(mtcars) |>   mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, color = "#6b4f2c")

title: Getting started
text: Marks stack. Add fitted line points piping another mark:
code: vplot(mtcars) |>   mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg) |>   mark_smooth(x = wt, y = mpg, method = "lm")

title: Getting started
text: facet_wrap() splits plot small multiples variable, theme_*() restyles whole thing:
code: vplot(mtcars) |>   mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, color = factor(cyl)) |>   facet_wrap(~cyl) |>   theme_minimal()

title: Getting started
text: vellumplot plot spec: object describing plot, picture. Nothing drawn print render , can examine built summary():
code: vplot(mtcars) |>   mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, color = hp) |>   summary() #> <PlotSpec> 32x11 (11 columns), page 6x4 in #> #> ── layers #> • mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, color = hp)

title: Getting started
text: render_plot() writes plot disk. format follows file extension, raster .png, vector .svg .pdf: spec produces every format; see one scene, three outputs convenience.
code: p <- vplot(mtcars) |> mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg)  render_plot(p, "cars.png")   # raster render_plot(p, "cars.pdf")   # vector

title: Getting started
text: Hand pipeline as_widget() becomes self-contained HTML widget: hover tooltips, click selection, brushing, pan/zoom, Shiny server. interactive bits declared mark: tooltip point shows hover, data_id identity selection. whole arc: spec printed, saved PDF, turned widget, without rewriting destination.
code: df <- data.frame(wt = mtcars$wt, mpg = mtcars$mpg, model = rownames(mtcars))  vplot(df) |>   mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, color = mpg, tooltip = model, data_id = model) |>   scale_color_continuous() |>   as_widget()

title: A critical appraisal of grid
text: helps concrete strengths, substantial reason ecosystem exists . real scene layout model. Unlike base graphics, grid represents plot objects (grobs) arranged inside system nested regions. makes complex, reusable graphics possible. faceted plot aligned panels, shared axes, common legend layout problem, grid engine solve rather pile manual coordinate arithmetic. Viewports. viewport grid’s central good idea. rectangular region coordinate systems, clipping, layout context, viewports nest. Drawing inside viewport xscale/yscale means can work data coordinates one place normalized coordinates another, without recomputing transforms hand. Almost everything expressive ggplot2’s rendered output rests . unit system. grid can express length centimetres, fraction current region, width string, width another grob, data-native coordinates, mix . able say “make margin wide widest axis label” powerful, simpler model capture . Device independence. grid scene targets abstract device, code renders screen, PDF, PNG, SVG. engine handles clipping, colour, display list; device handles rasterization. separation clean one plotting call can produce many output formats. enabled ecosystem. clearest evidence grid good engine everything built . ggplot2 one successful data-analysis tools language, grammar graphics compiled grobs viewports. grid deserves large share credit. None follows read denying points. critique narrower: grid strong engine demanding interface, distance two difficulty lives.

title: A critical appraisal of grid
text: ecosystem excellent high-level APIs capable low-level primitives, layer thin. top, write: bottom, manipulate grobs, units, viewports directly. comfortable middle ground requests fall two: “move legend inside third panel don’t clip ”, “align annotation rendered axis title”, “reserve space custom element”. grammar-level questions really primitive-level questions either, answering usually means dropping semantic layer implementation details: grob names, viewport paths, gtable cells, clipping flags. descent recurring theme. plot doesn’t exactly want, fix often lives layer two working.

Unit expressions can mix absolute, relative, text-derived, grob-derived measurements, many values knowable layout device-rendering time. inherent problem (string width font device exist), means questions like “panel width?” “measurement forced margin?” difficult answer outside. power opacity come place: lengths resolve late.

what: Clipping and coordinate systems can surprise

Underneath, several distinct coordinate spaces coexist (data, panel, plot, page, physical device), implicit. generally learn one trying something seeing lands. spaces real well-defined; surfaced first-class, named concepts, working across trial--error needs .

Together mean setting something ordinary consistent font line width across composed figure can require understanding one model. single cascading style layer spans plot types composition.

what: Performance scales with the object tree

text: Output depends target device (screen, PDF, cairo, ragg, quartz, ), font metrics, antialiasing, text shaping, rasterization can differ . variation unavoidable different backends render scene. assumptions given render made (fonts resolved, metrics used, rasterized) hard inspect, makes reproducing figure exactly across machines harder one like.

text: ggplot2 worth singling shows split clearly. grammar (data, aesthetics, geoms, stats, scales, coordinates, facets, themes) excellent, great majority plots never touch anything . ggplot object becomes rendered layout, grid-level characteristics reappear: legend placement can turn grob manipulation, facet customization gtable editing, panel-specific annotation stays awkward, composition reaches external package. ggplot2 solves grammar statistical graphics well. grammar graphical layout composition part remains harder high-level API suggests.

code: Easy things are very easy. Medium-hard things are surprisingly hard. Hard things require knowing internals.

title: One scene, three outputs
text: vellumplot plot spec. compiled vellum scene (concrete, laid-tree drawing primitives) single generic, as_vellum_scene(). Everything downstream consumes scene: vellum’s render() writes scene file, choosing raster vector extension. vellumwidget’s as_widget() turns scene HTML widget. call as_vellum_scene() hood. one seam object can go three places without rewritten.

title: One scene, three outputs
text: Nothing drawn. p description. can inspect , pass around, store , decide fate later: Notice tooltip data_id already mark. mean nothing static render everything widget. Carrying spec start lets one object serve .
code: df <- data.frame(wt = mtcars$wt, mpg = mtcars$mpg, model = rownames(mtcars))  p <- vplot(df) |>   mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, color = mpg, tooltip = model, data_id = model) |>   scale_color_continuous() |>   labs(title = "Fuel economy") summary(p) #> <PlotSpec> 32x3 (3 columns), page 6x4 in #> #> ── layers #> • mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, color = mpg)

title: One scene, three outputs
text: Printing spec draws active device; chunk , path raster file takes.  write disk PNG:
code: p render_plot(p, "fuel.png")          # tiny-skia raster render_plot(p, "fuel.png", dpi = 300)  # override the authored resolution

title: One scene, three outputs
text: identical spec, sent vector extension, rendered different backend (hand-rolled SVG writer, krilla PDF) change plot code: layout computed walked backend, raster vector versions plot, two approximations . point positions, text metrics, legend placement identical.
code: render_plot(p, "fuel.svg")   # vector SVG render_plot(p, "fuel.pdf")   # vector PDF

title: One scene, three outputs
text: p, handed as_widget(), compiles scene emits SVG plus per-element table, wired hover, selection, brushing, pan/zoom, client-side Shiny: tooltip data_id declared back spec widget reads. Three layers agreeing one contract: vellumplot declares channels, vellum carries scene, vellumwidget acts .
code: as_widget(p)

title: One scene, three outputs
text: payoff fewer keystrokes. one spec one compiled scene: outputs drift. raster figure interactive counterpart guaranteed show plot, scene. decision deferred. can write analysis code returns spec let calling context (Quarto chunk, Shiny app, batch job writing PDFs) decide realise . Bare vellum scenes get treatment. as_widget() render() accept hand-built vellum scene , bespoke visual made grobs travels three roads grammar plot.

what: Going lower: a bare vellum scene
title: One scene, three outputs
text: three-way rendering exists one layer , without grammar. vellum scene renders three formats directly: grammar vellumplot , end, convenient way produce one scenes. Whether scene came vplot() raw grobs, destinations .
code: s <- vl_scene(4, 3) |>   draw(circle_grob(r = 0.3, gp = vl_gpar(fill = "tomato", col = NA)))  render(s, "out.png")   # raster   (tiny-skia) render(s, "out.svg")   # vector   (hand-rolled SVG) render(s, "out.pdf")   # vector   (krilla) as_widget(s)           # interactive widget

title: One scene, three outputs
text: vellum ecosystem: layers connect. Performance big data: compiling buys data large.

what: Where the time goes, and doesn’t
title: Performance and big data
text: grid-based stack, plot’s display list replayed every resize, text measured whatever device current, point grob engine walks draw time. vellum moves work Rust: metrics available construction time, layout single explicit pass, one render walk emits chosen backend. consequence performance R-side cost plot building spec, cheap; drawing cost paid , compiled code. handles moderately large plots well. amount engine speed helps plot asks renderer draw two million individual circles SVG: file alone enormous, marks land pixel. problem datashade() solves.

title: Performance and big data
text: datashade() bins points grid size output image, counts many land cell, colours cells density. returns single raster, one image, instead millions marks. binning one-pass aggregation Rust, cost scales number pixels, number points. Ten thousand points ten million points cost almost draw, output -sized image either way. vellumplot, mark_datashade() mark like . million points, drawn density field:  mark_datashade() takes width height (aggregation grid, default 400 × 300), colors ramp, , controls counts map colour. default = "eq_hist" equalises histogram dense cells wash rest structure. aggregation available vellum datashade(), returns raster_grob place scene . Use building bespoke visual rather grammar plot:  datashade() bins xlim × ylim (defaulting data range), draw inside viewport whose xscale/yscale match limits image lines axes add around .
code: set.seed(1) n <- 1e6 df <- data.frame(   x = rnorm(n),   y = rnorm(n) * 0.5 + rnorm(n) )  vplot(df) |>   mark_datashade(x = x, y = y) set.seed(1) n <- 1e6 x <- rnorm(n) y <- x * 0.5 + rnorm(n)  vl_scene(6, 4.5, bg = "white") |>   push(vl_viewport(xscale = range(x), yscale = range(y))) |>   draw(datashade(x, y, width = 450, height = 300,                  colors = c("#fde0dd", "#7a0177")))

title: Performance and big data
text: vellumplot, mark_datashade() mark like . million points, drawn density field:  mark_datashade() takes width height (aggregation grid, default 400 × 300), colors ramp, , controls counts map colour. default = "eq_hist" equalises histogram dense cells wash rest structure.
code: set.seed(1) n <- 1e6 df <- data.frame(   x = rnorm(n),   y = rnorm(n) * 0.5 + rnorm(n) )  vplot(df) |>   mark_datashade(x = x, y = y)

title: Performance and big data
text: aggregation available vellum datashade(), returns raster_grob place scene . Use building bespoke visual rather grammar plot:  datashade() bins xlim × ylim (defaulting data range), draw inside viewport whose xscale/yscale match limits image lines axes add around .
code: set.seed(1) n <- 1e6 x <- rnorm(n) y <- x * 0.5 + rnorm(n)  vl_scene(6, 4.5, bg = "white") |>   push(vl_viewport(xscale = range(x), yscale = range(y))) |>   draw(datashade(x, y, width = 450, height = 300,                  colors = c("#fde0dd", "#7a0177")))

title: Performance and big data
text: decides cell counts become colour, knob changes see: "eq_hist" (default): histogram equalisation; best revealing structure across wide range densities. "log": log count; gentler compression equalisation. "cbrt": cube root; gentler still. "linear": raw counts; useful density fairly uniform. colors vector colours forming ramp sparse dense, weight lets accumulate summed quantity per cell instead plain count, weighted density.
code: # emphasise the tails with log scaling, custom ramp vplot(df) |>   mark_datashade(x = x, y = y, how = "log",                  colors = c("#f7fbff", "#08306b"))

title: Performance and big data
text: rule thumb pixel count. scatter points plot pixels, tens thousands , overplotting means marks invisible anyway, datashade() shows density hiding rendering constant time. , plain mark_point() fine keeps every point individually addressable (matters interactivity; datashaded image one raster, thousand hoverable points).

what: Choosing an output format at scale
title: Performance and big data
text: format render interacts data size. raster target (.png) fixed-size grid pixels regardless much data went . vector target (.svg, .pdf) stores every mark element, half-million-point scatter produces half-million-element file. large data, prefer raster output, datashade first even vector output single embedded image. See one scene, three outputs spec reaches format.

what: Diagnosing a slow or heavy scene
title: Performance and big data
text: vellum ships couple tools plot larger slower expect: scenes expensive sub-tree drawn repeatedly, viewport can cache rendered content vl_viewport(..., cache = TRUE), work done reused.
code: why_size(s)             # explain what is driving a scene's rendered size vl_clear_render_cache() # drop cached render state

title: Performance and big data
text: engine (scene graph, layout, render) runs Rust, per-plot R overhead building spec. datashade() / mark_datashade() render arbitrarily many points time proportional image size, aggregating density raster drawing. colors shape density reads; weight gives weighted densities. large data, prefer raster output datashade going vector.

title: Authors and Citation
text: David Schoch. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Schoch D (2026). vellumverse: Easily Install Load 'Vellum' Graphics Ecosystem. R package version 0.3.0, https://r-vellum.github.io/vellumverse/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {vellumverse: Easily Install and Load the 'Vellum' Graphics Ecosystem},   author = {David Schoch},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.3.0},   url = {https://r-vellum.github.io/vellumverse/}, }

title: The vellum graphics ecosystem
text: vellumverse installs loads vellum graphics ecosystem one step, way tidyverse data science. packages carry shared metaphor: vellum parchment, vellumplot pen, vellumwidget annotation revealed page.

title: The vellum graphics ecosystem
text: ecosystem compiles Rust crate (vellum), need Rust toolchain (cargo/rustc) alongside R. :
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("r-vellum/vellumverse")

title: The vellum graphics ecosystem
text: single call attaches three core packages, can go data frame interactive plot one pipeline: vplot(), mark_point(), scales come vellumplot; as_widget() comes vellumwidget; compile vellum scene.
code: library(vellumverse) #> ── Attaching packages ──────────────────────────────────── vellumverse 0.1.0 ── #> ✔ vellum 0.1.0   ✔ vellumplot 0.1.0   ✔ vellumwidget 0.1.0 library(vellumverse)  df <- data.frame(wt = mtcars$wt, mpg = mtcars$mpg, model = rownames(mtcars))  vplot(df) |>                                     # vellumplot: grammar of graphics   mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, color = mpg,              tooltip = model, data_id = model) |>   scale_color_continuous() |>   as_widget()                                    # vellumwidget: interactive widget

title: The vellum graphics ecosystem
text: load packages without startup banner, set options(vellumverse.quiet = TRUE) attaching.
code: vellumverse_packages()    # the packages vellumverse bundles vellumverse_conflicts()   # functions masked across the ecosystem

what: The core of the vellum ecosystem — vellumverse-core
title: The core of the vellum ecosystem — vellumverse-core
text: vellumverse bundles packages make vellum graphics ecosystem can installed loaded one step, way tidyverse bundles data-science packages. Attaching vellumverse attaches three core packages:

title: The core of the vellum ecosystem — vellumverse-core
text: vellum, low-level graphics backend (Rust scene graph, unit/layout engine, multi-backend renderer). vellumplot, pipe-first grammar graphics compiles inspectable plot spec vellum scene. vellumwidget, client-side interactive HTML widgets scenes vellum vellumplot produce.

what: vellumverse: Easily Install and Load the 'Vellum' Graphics Ecosystem — vellumverse-package
title: vellumverse: Easily Install and Load the 'Vellum' Graphics Ecosystem — vellumverse-package
text: 'vellum' ecosystem set packages drawing R share scene model common design: 'vellum' (low-level graphics backend Rust engine), 'vellumplot' (pipe-first grammar graphics top ), 'vellumwidget' (client-side interactive HTML widgets scenes produce). 'vellumverse' meta-package installs loads together, spirit 'tidyverse'.

title: vellumverse: Easily Install and Load the 'Vellum' Graphics Ecosystem — vellumverse-package
text: Maintainer: David Schoch david.schoch@cynkra.com Authors: David Schoch david.schoch@cynkra.com

what: Conflicts between the vellum ecosystem and other packages — vellumverse_conflicts
title: Conflicts between the vellum ecosystem and other packages — vellumverse_conflicts
text: vellumverse_conflicts() lists every function exported core vellum-ecosystem package masked , masks, function name attached elsewhere search path. equivalent tidyverse::tidyverse_conflicts(), also reported automatically vellumverse attached.

title: Conflicts between the vellum ecosystem and other packages — vellumverse_conflicts
code: vellumverse_conflicts()

title: Conflicts between the vellum ecosystem and other packages — vellumverse_conflicts
text: S3 object class "vellumverse_conflicts", named list mapping conflicted function packages export (search-path order). print method.

title: Conflicts between the vellum ecosystem and other packages — vellumverse_conflicts
code: vellumverse_conflicts() #> ── Conflicts ──────────────────────────────────────── vellumverse_conflicts() ── #> ✖ vellumplot::linear_gradient masks vellum #> ✖ vellumplot::md              masks vellum #> ✖ vellumplot::radial_gradient masks vellum #> ✖ vellumplot::sketch          masks vellum

what: List the core vellum-ecosystem packages — vellumverse_packages
title: List the core vellum-ecosystem packages — vellumverse_packages
text: vellumverse_packages() returns names packages library(vellumverse) attaches.

title: List the core vellum-ecosystem packages — vellumverse_packages
code: vellumverse_packages(include_self = TRUE)

title: List the core vellum-ecosystem packages — vellumverse_packages
text: include_self Whether include vellumverse list.

title: List the core vellum-ecosystem packages — vellumverse_packages
text: character vector package names.

title: List the core vellum-ecosystem packages — vellumverse_packages
code: vellumverse_packages() #> [1] "vellum"       "vellumplot"   "vellumwidget" "vellumverse"

title: vellumverse 0.3.0
text: Bumped pinned ecosystem versions latest releases: vellum (>= 0.4.0), vellumplot (>= 0.5.0), vellumwidget (>= 0.5.0).

title: vellumverse 0.2.1
text: Maintenance release. Declared stats dependency (used reporting ecosystem conflicts), dropped unused rlang dependency keep meta-package dependency-light, removed dead startup argument vellumverse_conflicts() print method.

title: vellumverse 0.2.0
text: Adopted vellum’s renamed vl_* graphics primitives (grid collision fix).

title: vellumverse 0.1.0
text: First release. library(vellumverse) attaches core vellum graphics ecosystem (vellum, vellumplot, vellumwidget) one step reports versions loaded. vellumverse_packages() lists bundled packages vellumverse_conflicts() surfaces functions masked across ecosystem. Set options(vellumverse.quiet = TRUE) attach without startup banner.
